POL-357.title: IMPLEMENT: Multi-language graph adapter architecture
POL-357.body:
## Objective

Refactor the Polaris graph backend from a TypeScript/JavaScript-only extraction system into a language-adapter architecture with a graph capability registry and graceful degradation for unsupported languages.

## Context

Analysis from POL-356 (Analyze multi-language graph extraction and resolver architecture). The phase-one graph backend implemented in POL-345 provides a solid language-neutral store, query API, and governance layer. However, the parser/extraction layer is hardcoded to TypeScript/JavaScript with no adapter abstraction, and unsupported language files are silently skipped with a warning.

This cluster introduces the architectural foundation for multi-language support without implementing individual language adapters yet. Full analysis and architecture spec: docs/Polaris/spec/multi-language-graph-architecture.md.

## Goal

Deliver a language-adapter-driven graph system where:
1. Language support is governed by a registered adapter interface, not a hardcoded type union
2. Unsupported language files create file-level graph nodes rather than disappearing silently
3. A capability registry tracks and reports graph coverage by language in graph build output

## Scope

- src/graph/adapter/**
- src/graph/parser/loader.ts
- src/graph/parser/extract.ts
- src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts
- src/graph/parser/extract.test.ts
- src/graph/resolver/resolve-imports.ts
- src/cli/graph.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] LanguageAdapter interface and AdapterRegistry defined in src/graph/adapter/
- [ ] TypeScript/JavaScript extraction logic migrated into src/graph/adapter/typescript-javascript/
- [ ] Adapter registry drives file extension detection
- [ ] Unsupported language files produce file-level FILE nodes with partialCoverage: true metadata
- [ ] GraphCapabilityRegistry implemented and attached to ExtractionPipelineResult
- [ ] graph build output includes per-language coverage report
- [ ] All existing TypeScript/JavaScript extraction behavior preserved exactly
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass
- [ ] npx vitest run src/graph/ passes

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/graph/
- npm run polaris -- graph build --dry-run --json
- npm run polaris -- graph build --json

## Ordering

Depends on POL-345 implementation being merged. Children execute in order: POL-358 → POL-359 → POL-360.

## Non-goals

- Do not implement language-specific adapters for Svelte, Dart, Swift, Kotlin, Python, or other languages
- Do not implement cross-language edge resolution
- Do not change the graph store schema, query API, or governance layer
- Do not make graph data authoritative for dispatch or completion evidence
- Do not implement incremental or watcher-based rebuilds

POL-345.title: IMPLEMENT: Add Polaris function-level repository graph backend

POL-356.title: Analyze multi-language graph extraction and resolver architecture

POL-358.title: LanguageAdapter interface and TypeScript/JavaScript adapter migration
POL-358.body:
## Objective

Define the LanguageAdapter interface and AdapterRegistry, then migrate all existing TypeScript/JavaScript extraction logic into the first registered adapter.

## Context

First child of POL-357 (IMPLEMENT: Multi-language graph adapter architecture). Analysis in POL-356 identified that src/graph/parser/loader.ts hardcodes SupportedParserLanguage = 'typescript' | 'javascript', extract.ts uses TS/JS-specific Tree-sitter node names, and pipeline.ts uses a hardcoded extension list.

## Goal

1. New src/graph/adapter/types.ts — LanguageAdapter interface and AdapterRegistry interface
2. New src/graph/adapter/registry.ts — concrete AdapterRegistry implementation
3. New src/graph/adapter/typescript-javascript/ — existing TS/JS extraction logic migrated here
4. src/graph/parser/loader.ts — refactored; Tree-sitter loading moves into the TS/JS adapter
5. src/graph/parser/extract.ts — refactored; logic moves into the TS/JS adapter
6. src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts — updated to dispatch via adapter registry
7. src/cli/graph.ts — SUPPORTED_SOURCE_EXTENSIONS replaced with registry.getSupportedExtensions()

## Scope

- src/graph/adapter/types.ts
- src/graph/adapter/registry.ts
- src/graph/adapter/typescript-javascript/**
- src/graph/parser/loader.ts
- src/graph/parser/extract.ts
- src/graph/parser/extract.test.ts
- src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts
- src/cli/graph.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] LanguageAdapter interface defined with: languageId, fileExtensions, confidence, limitations, extractSymbols(), resolveImportSpecifier()
- [ ] AdapterRegistry supports register(), getForExtension(), getSupportedExtensions(), getAll()
- [ ] TypeScript/JavaScript adapter registered and handles .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx, .mjs, .cjs
- [ ] pipeline.ts dispatches extraction via adapter registry; no hardcoded language check remains
- [ ] SUPPORTED_SOURCE_EXTENSIONS removed from src/cli/graph.ts
- [ ] All existing extraction tests pass
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

No dependencies — executes first in cluster.

## Non-goals

- Do not add any language adapter other than TypeScript/JavaScript
- Do not implement file-level graceful degradation (Child 2)
- Do not implement GraphCapabilityRegistry (Child 3)
- Do not change the graph store schema or query API

POL-359.title: File-level graceful degradation for unsupported languages
POL-359.body:
## Objective

Update the extraction pipeline so that files with no registered adapter produce file-level graph nodes rather than being silently skipped.

## Context

Second child of POL-357. Depends on POL-358 (adapter registry) being Done.

## Goal

For any file whose extension has no registered adapter:
1. Create a FILE node with language = 'unsupported:<ext>'
2. Attach node metadata { partialCoverage: true, reason: 'no-adapter' }
3. Do not attempt symbol extraction for these files
4. Accumulate fallback file data in pipeline result for Child 3

## Scope

- src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Files with no registered adapter create a FILE node with language = 'unsupported:<ext>'
- [ ] FILE node metadata includes { partialCoverage: true, reason: 'no-adapter' }
- [ ] No symbol nodes or edges created for unsupported-language files
- [ ] ExtractionPipelineResult includes fallbackFiles: { path: string; ext: string }[]
- [ ] Resolver does not attempt import resolution against file-level-only nodes
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/graph/

## Ordering

Depends on POL-358 being Done.

## Non-goals

- Do not implement GraphCapabilityRegistry (Child 3)
- Do not attempt symbol-level extraction for unsupported files
- Do not change the graph store schema
- Do not add new language adapters

POL-360.title: GraphCapabilityRegistry and graph build coverage report
POL-360.body:
## Objective

Implement GraphCapabilityRegistry that accumulates per-language extraction outcomes during a graph build, and surface a structured coverage report in graph build CLI output.

## Context

Third and final child of POL-357. Depends on POL-358 and POL-359 being Done.

## Goal

1. New src/graph/capability/index.ts — GraphCapabilityRegistry class and GraphCapabilityReport type
2. src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts — integrate registry; attach report to ExtractionPipelineResult
3. src/cli/graph.ts — extend graph build JSON with extraction.capability; extend text output with per-language table

## Scope

- src/graph/capability/index.ts
- src/graph/parser/pipeline.ts
- src/cli/graph.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GraphCapabilityReport type matches shape in docs/Polaris/spec/multi-language-graph-architecture.md Section 3
- [ ] GraphCapabilityRegistry accumulates filesDiscovered, filesSymbolLevel, filesFileLevel, filesFailed, symbolsExtracted, warnings per language
- [ ] ExtractionPipelineResult.capability field present and populated
- [ ] graph build --json output includes extraction.capability object
- [ ] graph build text output includes per-language coverage table
- [ ] symbolLevelPercent and totalCoveragePercent computed correctly
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass

## Validation

- npm run build
- npm test
- npx vitest run src/graph/
- npm run polaris -- graph build --json

## Ordering

Depends on POL-358 and POL-359 being Done.

## Non-goals

- Do not integrate capability report into Analyst or Medic context packets
- Do not add new language adapters
- Do not change the graph store schema or query API
